expectations.text: JWT verification uses an established library (e.g., jsonwebtoken) with the algorithm explicitly pinned to HS256 — does not accept algorithms from the token header (no 'alg: none' / algorithm-confusion vulnerability)
expectations.evidence: Uses jose library with explicit `algorithms: [...policy.algorithms]` where policy.algorithms is `readonly ["HS256"]`; dependency notes state jose 'requires explicit `algorithms` allowlist' and does not accept 'alg: none'. Comment: 'HS256 only — guards against alg-confusion (V9.1.2)'.

expectations.text: The Authorization header is parsed defensively — handles missing header, malformed header, missing 'Bearer ' prefix, multiple spaces, and case variants without crashing
expectations.evidence: extractBearerToken function checks Array.isArray, typeof string, trims whitespace, uses case-insensitive regex `^bearer$/i`, splits on `/\s+/` (handles multiple spaces), and returns null for all malformed cases rather than throwing.

expectations.text: Middleware emits 401 (or 403 where appropriate) with a generic message; the response body does not leak the underlying jwt.verify error string, the token, or any stack content
expectations.evidence: sendUnauthorized returns `res.status(401).json({ error: 'unauthorized' })` with generic message; errors are classified into internal reason codes for logs only; comment 'Generic message — never leak which check failed (V16.5.1)'.

expectations.text: Token contents (payload) are NEVER logged or echoed back to the client; logs use structured key/value with user identifier only after verification succeeds
expectations.evidence: Logs only contain reason, request_id, path, method on rejection; on acceptance logs user_id (sub) and issuer after successful verification and projection. Raw payload/token never logged. Comment: 'never the token, never the secret'.

expectations.text: Verified-payload extraction is restricted to expected fields (e.g., sub/iss/exp); the middleware does not blindly attach the full decoded payload to req.user
expectations.evidence: projectAuthenticatedUser only lifts `sub` (as id) and `iss` (as issuer) into a frozen AuthenticatedUser object; comment 'Request Surface Minimization — only `sub` and `iss` are lifted'.

expectations.text: Process startup or initialization fails fast / logs an error if JWT_SECRET is missing or empty — middleware does not silently degrade to 'always allow' or 'always deny by accident'
expectations.evidence: loadJwtVerificationPolicy throws `Error('JWT_SECRET is not set')` if missing/empty and also throws if under 32 bytes. Called at startup: 'Build once, at startup, so any config error fails the boot — not a request.'

expectations.text: Type-safe shape: req.user (or augmented Request) has an explicit TypeScript type rather than 'any'
expectations.evidence: Explicit `AuthenticatedUser` interface with readonly `id: string` and optional `issuer?: string`; `declare module 'express-serve-static-core' { interface Request { user?: AuthenticatedUser } }` augments Express Request type.

expectations.text: Output includes a Securability Notes block listing SSEM attributes enforced, ASVS references, trust boundaries, dependencies with versions, and trade-offs
expectations.evidence: Dedicated 'Securability Notes' section lists SSEM attributes (Authenticity, Integrity, Accountability, Resilience, Modifiability/Testability), ASVS references (V9.1.1, V9.1.2, V9.1.3, V9.2.1, V9.2.3, V10.3.1, V10.3.3, V16.5.1, V7.2.1, V6.8.2), trust boundaries, dependencies (jose@^5), and trade-offs.

expectations.text: Token replay/expiry handling is explicit (jwt.verify enforces exp; clock-skew tolerance is set or noted as a trade-off)
expectations.evidence: Policy has `clockToleranceSeconds: 5` passed to jose as `clockTolerance`; comment 'jose enforces exp/nbf automatically'; trade-off notes 'Clock tolerance is 5 seconds — tighten or loosen per deployment' and acknowledges no revocation/jti denylist (V7.4.1) as a known gap.

expectations.text: Middleware is exported as a function factory or pure function so it is unit-testable without spinning up an Express app
expectations.evidence: `buildJwtAuthMiddleware(opts: JwtAuthMiddlewareOptions)` is a factory that returns the middleware; accepts injected `logger` and `verify` function (`opts.verify ?? jwtVerify`) for testability; extractBearerToken is a separate pure function.
